title: Error Indicator Readout - DINGLI JCPT0607DCS Operator's Manual Page 31 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607DCS full fault code list (operator's manual page 31): Code 10 GENERAL ALARM ECU; Code 20 GENERAL ALARM TM1; Code 30 GENERAL ALARM PCU; Code 31 PCU CPU0 FAULT; Code 32 PCU CPU1 FAULT; Code 51 ECU ALARM; Code 52 PCU ALARM; Code 53 TM1 ALARM; Code 54 PRESS SENSOR ERROR — check pressure sensor wiring and sensor; Code 57 OVERLOAD — platform load exceeds rated capacity, reduce load; Code 58 POTHOLE — pothole protection activated, drive machine over pothole/edge slowly; Code 59 HIGH POSITION LIMIT — platform at maximum height limit; Code 110 ECU WATCHDOG FAULT — ECU internal error, restart machine; Code 111 CANBUS TIMEOUT 1 PCU CPU0 — communication fault between PCU and ECU, check CAN bus wiring; Code 112 CANBUS TIMEOUT 1 PCU CPU1 — same as 111 for CPU1 channel. Reset procedure: push in and pull out the red Emergency Stop button.

title: How to Fix Dingli JCPT0607A Fault Code 69 — Power Relay Sleep
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607A fault code 69 — Power Relay Sleep: The system entered sleep mode and the power relay was deactivated. This code also appears on JCPT1912DC. Cause: battery charge too low to maintain power relay energized, or key switch issue, or prolonged inactivity triggering sleep. Solutions: (1) Restart the machine by cycling the key switch off and back on. (2) Check battery charge level — charge batteries fully before operating. (3) Verify key switch operation and wiring. (4) Check power relay and its control circuit. If machine powers up and immediately shuts down, battery may be too low to sustain operation; fully charge before next use.

title: How to Fix Dingli JCPT1912DC Fault Code 69 — Power Relay Sleep
content_summary: Dingli JCPT1912DC fault code 69 — Power Relay Sleep: Same as JCPT0607A code 69. System entered sleep mode and power relay deactivated. Solutions: (1) Restart via key switch cycle. (2) Fully charge batteries. (3) Verify key switch and power relay. The JCPT1912DC is a DC-powered 19ft scissor lift. If error persists after full charge, inspect the power relay coil and control wiring from the ECU. Check for corrosion on battery terminals and main power connections.

title: Error Indicator Readout; List Of Fault Codes - DINGLI JCPT0307 Operators Manual Page 29 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0307 fault code list from operator's manual (page 29). The LED diagnostic readout on JCPT0307 displays fault codes. When code 'LL' appears: push in and pull out the red Emergency Stop button to reset the system. Fault code categories: ECU alarms (10-series), PCU alarms (30-series), module communication timeouts (100-series). Reset procedure for most codes: key switch cycle or Emergency Stop reset. The JCPT0307 is a compact 3m x 0.7m electric scissor lift. Fault code access: observe 2-digit or 3-digit number on LED display on ground control box.

title: Fault Code Chart (After Serial Number 17407) - Genie GS-2046 Service Manual Page 50 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie scissor lift fault code chart applicable to models GS-1930, GS-1932, GS-2032, GS-2632, GS-2046, GS-2646, GS-3246 (after serial number 17407). Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) generated by Genie SmartLink Diagnostic Systems: Code 01 Platform ECM error — replace platform ECM; Code 02 Undefined platform DIP switch settings — check DIP switch configuration; Code 03 Chassis up/down switch closed at startup — switch stuck, inspect switch; Code 18 Pothole guard failure — pothole guard not deploying correctly, inspect mechanism; Code 42 Coil error forward drive — check forward drive coil and wiring; Code 43 Coil error reverse drive; Code 44 Coil error up; Code 45 Coil error down; Code 46 Coil error right steer; Code 47 Coil error left steer or brake; Code 68 Low Voltage — battery below minimum operating voltage, charge batteries. Operational Indicator Codes (OIC): E1 Emergency Stop activated; E4 Low Battery; E6 Tilt Sensor active; E12/E13 Control Communication fault. Total: 272 DTCs and 6 OIC codes in full Genie GSDS system.

title: Troubleshooting with Scissor Error Codes | Genie
content_summary: Official Genie troubleshooting page for scissor lift error codes. The Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System (GSDS) generates Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) indicating device or circuit malfunctions. Five system categories: hydraulic, battery/power, user interface, sensors/switches, and controls. Fault codes on ground control visible from page 182 of Genie Scissor Service and Repair Manual. Two code types: (1) Operational Indicator Codes (OIC) appearing during normal operation; (2) DTCs indicating malfunctions. Common OIC codes: E1 (Emergency Stop engaged), E4 (Low Battery — charge batteries), E6 (Tilt Sensor — move machine to level ground), E12/E13 (Control Communication fault — check control box wiring). Reset procedure: lower platform completely to ground, turn off machine, engage override mode on control panel, turn machine back on. 272 total DTCs across Genie scissor lift range.

title: Genie ECU Fault Codes GEN 5 — Hindley Electronics
content_summary: Genie ECU GEN 5 fault code reference document from Hindley Electronics. Contains fault codes for Genie scissor lift ECU generation 5 control systems including models GS-1932, GS-2032, GS-2632, GS-3246, GS-4047. Lists ECU fault codes with causes and corrective actions. GEN 5 ECU is used in mid-2000s to early 2010s Genie scissors. This PDF is a technical reference for field service technicians to diagnose ECU-generated faults without the full service manual. Complement to Genie's official service manual fault code appendix.

title: Genie ECU Fault Codes GEN 6 — Hindley Electronics
content_summary: Genie ECU GEN 6 fault code reference document. Contains fault codes for newer Genie scissor lift ECU generation 6 control systems. GEN 6 ECU introduced on later model Genie scissors. Lists all ECU fault codes with numeric identifiers, fault descriptions, likely causes, and recommended corrective actions for each code. Used for diagnosing Genie SmartLink system faults on machines equipped with GEN 6 controller. Key reference for distinguishing GEN 5 vs GEN 6 behavior and fault code numbering differences.

title: Genie GS-3246 GS-2646 GS-2046 Service Manual Part 48339 | Genie Official
content_summary: Official Genie service and repair manual part number 48339 (first edition, third printing rev C2) covering models GS-3246, GS-2646, GS-2046. Published by Genie Industries. Contains: machine specifications, theory of operation, inspection and maintenance schedules, fault code chart (page 50+), troubleshooting procedures for hydraulic system, electrical system, and drive system. Fault code chart begins after serial number 17407. Hydraulic system pressure specs. Electrical schematic and wiring diagrams. Battery maintenance and charging procedures. Scissor arm inspection criteria. Platform leveling and tilt sensor calibration.

title: JLG Fault Codes, DTC Lookup Codes And Other Troubleshooting | JLG
content_summary: Official JLG guide to fault codes and troubleshooting. A JLG fault code is a message from the machine's ECU indicating a currently detected problem. How to read codes: JLG offers three machine analyzer tools — (1) tethered cable analyzer, (2) mobile handheld analyzer, (3) Bluetooth-enabled analyzer for select scissor lift models, plus remote diagnostics via ClearSky fleet management platform. Troubleshooting steps: (1) Identify DTC group from highest first two digits; (2) Look up code in Service & Maintenance Manual table; (3) Perform recommended checks — visual inspection, component testing, or corrective action. For wiring faults: check harness and connector pins for shorts; if harness OK, controller may need replacement. Check all connectors for security. Complete Knowledge Base with DTC solutions at jlg.com/directaccess. JLG service manuals available through Online Express portal.

title: JLG 450AJ Series 2 Fault Codes: Flash Code 3-3 Troubleshooting | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG 450AJ Series 2 flash code 3-3 troubleshooting discussion. Flash code 3-3 on JLG 450AJ Series 2 (year 2004) causes machine blocking and 2-speed not working. Suspected cause: tilt sensor fault or tilt sensor calibration issue. Flash codes on JLG 450AJ are read from MIL indicator light on platform console and control board inside ground control box. For JLG 450AJ: check tilt sensor operation and wiring. Tilt sensor location: mounted on chassis frame. Calibration procedure required after sensor replacement. Also mentioned: JLG 45E receiving flash fault code 4 flashes for motor issue. Reference: JLG service manuals downloadable at jlg.com service & parts section.

title: Fault Codes - JLG 450A Series II Service and Maintenance Manual Page 343 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG 450A Series II fault codes table from the official service and maintenance manual (page 343). Complete diagnostic fault code list for JLG 450A Series II articulating boom lift. DTC groups organized by first two digits. Codes cover: engine system faults, drive system faults, boom/platform control faults, sensor faults (tilt, load, position), communication faults. Each code has: code number, description, group category, and recommended action. Service manual also contains troubleshooting decision trees and electrical/hydraulic schematics. The 450A Series II is an engine-powered articulating boom with 45ft working height. Manual reference number available through JLG Online Express portal.

title: JLG Boom Lift Troubleshooting — Intella Parts
content_summary: JLG boom lift troubleshooting guide covering common fault scenarios. Key troubleshooting steps: (1) For wiring faults — check power and control wiring for shorts in harness and connector pins; if harness tests OK, controller requires replacement. (2) Basket position sensors — inspect for dirt, damage, or loose wiring; clean or reconnect. (3) Joystick issues — check joystick calibration, ensure all connectors are secure. (4) System reset — cycle power off and on. (5) Boom/platform movement faults — check hydraulic pressure, solenoid valve operation, and relevant DTC. Models referenced: JLG 450AJ, 520AJ, 860SJ. General advice: always start from DTC group with highest first two digits, use JLG Knowledge Base at jlg.com/directaccess for model-specific DTC solutions.

title: JLG 450AJ Service and Maintenance Manual | Swift Equipment
content_summary: Full service and maintenance manual for JLG 450AJ articulating boom lift, hosted by Swift Equipment Australia. Contains: complete fault code table (DTC groups 1-9), electrical schematics, hydraulic schematics, troubleshooting flowcharts for all major systems, component replacement procedures, torque specifications, preventive maintenance intervals. Key sections: Section 2 System Theory (hydraulic/electrical overview), Section 3 Troubleshooting (DTC diagnosis), Section 4 Maintenance, Section 5 Repair procedures. Hydraulic system: engine-driven pump, flow divider for drive and boom circuits, pilot-operated load holding valves on boom cylinders. Electrical: CAN-bus control system with platform and ground ECMs, analyzer port on ground control box.

title: JLG E450AJ M450AJ Service Manual PDF | Simeri
content_summary: Service manual for JLG E450AJ (electric) and M450AJ (diesel/gas) articulating boom lifts. The E450AJ is the electric-powered variant of the 450AJ, featuring a 45ft working height, 500 lb platform capacity, electric drive and lift system. Manual includes: fault code tables for both E450AJ and M450AJ variants, CAN-bus diagnostic procedures, electric motor troubleshooting (E450AJ), engine troubleshooting (M450AJ), hydraulic system service, platform leveling system, oscillating axle system. Help Descriptions and Fault Flash Codes section (page 147 reference) covers all DTC codes with descriptions and corrective actions.

title: MANITOU Fault Codes DTC — Truck-Manuals.Net
content_summary: Manitou fault codes and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) reference list. Covers Manitou telehandlers and aerial work platforms including 180ATJ and other ATJ series. Common Manitou fault categories: transmission faults, engine faults, hydraulic system faults, sensor faults. Common issues reported: air leaks from Schrader valves or O-rings causing boom drift; oil leaks from wiper seals or damper shaft seals; lack of travel due to tight bushings or low hydraulic fluid levels. Most Manitou platform faults require authorized Manitou Support via local dealer network — dealer locator at manitou.com. Manitou Maintenance & Diagnostic Software (MDS) required for advanced fault code reading on newer machines. Key service intervals and fault indicators described.

title: Manitou Systems 180 ATJ Manuals | ManualsLib
content_summary: ManualsLib listing for Manitou Systems 180 ATJ aerial work platform manuals. The 180 ATJ is a diesel-powered articulated telescopic jib aerial work platform with 18m (59ft) working height. Available manuals include: Operator's Manual (free PDF download), Service Manual. Key specifications: max working height 18m, max outreach 11m, platform capacity 200kg. Fault code documentation for 180ATJ is included in the service manual. The 180ATJ uses a Deutz engine (EURO 3 variant) with its own ECU fault codes in addition to the Manitou platform control system codes. Electric circuit diagrams available separately (Scribd document 666148430).

title: Complete Manitou Error Codes List and Troubleshooting Guide — Ultimate Systems Blog
content_summary: Comprehensive guide to Manitou error codes and troubleshooting published 2025. Covers Manitou telehandlers, aerial work platforms, and forklifts including 180ATJ series. Fault code categories: engine ECU codes (Deutz, Perkins, Cummins depending on model), transmission codes, hydraulic system codes, safety system codes. Common codes and meanings listed with solutions. Manitou Maintenance & Diagnostic Software (MDS) required to read and clear fault codes on most models. Key troubleshooting steps: identify fault code from instrument cluster or MDS, check service manual for DTC description, perform listed checks (wiring, sensors, hydraulic pressure), clear code after repair. Dealer contact required for codes indicating safety system faults.

title: Manitou 180 ATJ EURO 3 Electric Circuit Diagram | Scribd
content_summary: Manitou 180 ATJ EURO 3 complete electrical circuit diagram. This document contains the full wiring schematic for the Manitou 180 ATJ with EURO 3 Deutz engine. Includes: main power distribution diagram, engine ECU wiring, platform control wiring, safety system wiring (overload sensor, tilt sensor, limit switches), boom and jib control circuit, drive system circuit, lighting circuit. Essential reference for electrical fault diagnosis on 180ATJ machines. EURO 3 emission control wiring included. Connector pin-out diagrams for main harness connectors.

title: Dingli JCPT1412DC JCPT1612DC Self-Propelled Scissor Lifts Operator's Manual | Advanced Access Platforms
content_summary: Dingli JCPT1412DC and JCPT1612DC operator's manual with maintenance information (2023). Covers: safety rules and regulations, machine controls and indicators, operating procedures, maintenance schedule, fault code readout and list. JCPT1412DC: 14ft (4.2m) platform height, 1412 model designation (14m height / 12m deck). DC = direct current (battery) powered. Control system: DCM (DC Motor controller). Fault code display: LED on ground control box and platform control box. Fault code reset: Emergency Stop button cycle or key switch cycle. Maintenance items: hydraulic oil check (every 250 hours), battery fluid check (weekly), scissor pivot pin lubrication (monthly), overload sensor calibration (annually).

title: Dingli Scissor Lifts Product Manuals — Dingli Global Official
content_summary: Official Dingli Global scissor lift product manuals compilation PDF. Contains operator manuals and maintenance information for multiple JCPT series models. Models covered include JCPT0607, JCPT0808, JCPT1012, JCPT1208, JCPT1412, JCPT1612, JCPT1912. For each model: specifications, controls description, safety systems, fault code list, maintenance schedule. Fault code section common to all DC models: codes in range 10-112 for ECU/PCU/TM1 alarms. Code 69 (Power Relay Sleep) applicable to all battery-powered models. Hydraulic system: manual lowering valve location, hydraulic oil type (HM46 hydraulic oil), oil capacity per model. Battery: 24V system on compact models, 48V on larger models.

title: Faults Scissor Lift Aerial Work Platform and Solutions | Cathay Lift
content_summary: Scissor lift aerial work platform common faults and solutions guide. Hydraulic system faults: (1) Platform won't lift or lifts slowly — check hydraulic oil level, inspect for leaks, check pump pressure, verify solenoid valve operation; (2) Platform drifts down when raised — check cylinder seals, check load holding valve, inspect hydraulic hose connections; (3) Uneven lifting — check for air in hydraulic system, bleed hydraulic lines, inspect cylinder synchronization. Electrical faults: (1) Machine won't power on — check battery charge, inspect key switch, check main fuse/circuit breaker; (2) Controls unresponsive — check control harness connections, inspect joystick, verify ECU power. Mechanical faults: (1) Scissor arm noise — inspect pivot pins and bushings, lubricate; (2) Machine won't drive — check drive motor, inspect drive controller, verify safety systems (tilt, pothole guard). Maintenance tips: weekly oil inspection, monthly greasing of all pivot points, annual hydraulic oil change.

title: Why the JLG Fault Codes List Improves Safety | Aerial Equipment Parts
content_summary: Article explaining how JLG's fault code system improves equipment safety and maintenance efficiency. JLG fault codes are organized into DTC groups: Group 1 (engine-related), Group 2 (drive system), Group 3 (platform/boom positioning), Group 4 (sensor faults), Group 5 (communication/CAN bus faults). Each code has a two-part identifier: group number + specific fault number (e.g., 3-3, 5-437). The fault code system allows technicians to pinpoint faults without disassembly. JLG Knowledge Base at jlg.com/directaccess provides searchable DTC lookup by model and code number. Regular fault code monitoring enables predictive maintenance — recurring codes indicate developing component failures before complete failure occurs.

title: JLG 2646 ES: Fault Codes 9-2 and 9-3 | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum discussion about JLG 2646 ES scissor lift fault codes 9-2 and 9-3. Fault code 9-2 and 9-3 on JLG 2646 ES — these are Group 9 codes related to the load management system or platform sensor faults. Community discussion includes diagnosis steps and solutions from experienced technicians. JLG 2646 ES is an electric scissor lift with 26ft working height, 46in deck width. Forum threads on Forkliftaction are community-sourced and include real-world field experience from operators and technicians. Reliability: 0.8 as forkliftaction.com is a reputable industry forum.

title: JLG 660SJ Error Code — Maintenance & Troubleshooting | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum discussion about JLG 660SJ telescopic boom lift error codes. JLG 660SJ is a telescopic boom with 66ft working height and 660 lb capacity. Forum thread covers specific error code(s) experienced, community diagnosis, and solutions. Boom lift error codes on JLG 660SJ follow same DTC group structure as other JLG products. Common issues on large telescopic booms: turntable rotation sensor faults, telescope extension sensor faults, outrigger/axle lock sensor faults. Forum threads provide practical field-level troubleshooting not always covered in the official service manual.

title: JLG Error Codes List — Diagnostic Fault / Trouble Codes (DTCs) for MEWPs | CodeReady
content_summary: Comprehensive JLG error codes database covering DTCs for MEWPs, lifts, and telehandlers. Organized by DTC group number. Group structure: groups 1-9 cover different system areas. Includes fault codes for JLG articulating booms (450AJ, 520AJ, 600AJ, 860SJ), telescopic booms (400S, 600S, 800S, 860SJ), scissor lifts (2032ES, 2646ES, 3246ES, 4046ES), and vertical masts. Each code entry includes: code number, fault description, possible cause, and corrective action. CodeReady is a third-party technical database aggregating manufacturer DTC information for construction and material handling equipment.

title: Hydraulic Aerial Lifts Maintenance 101 | Cylinders, Inc.
content_summary: Technical guide to preventing hydraulic problems in boom lift aerial work platforms. Key maintenance topics: (1) Hydraulic fluid — use correct grade (typically ISO 46 hydraulic oil), change every 1000 hours or annually, check level weekly; contamination causes premature pump and valve failure. (2) Cylinder seals — inspect for leaks at rod and barrel, replace if weeping; platform drift indicates internal cylinder leak or load holding valve fault. (3) Hoses and fittings — inspect for abrasion, cracking, chafing; replace hoses every 5-7 years regardless of appearance. (4) Hydraulic pump — listen for cavitation noise (whining), check inlet filter/strainer; cavitation causes rapid pump damage. (5) Control valves — solenoid valves: test coil resistance (typically 20-40 ohms), check for contamination sticking valve spool. (6) Pressure settings — main relief valve, secondary relief valves; check against service manual specifications. Diagnostic approach: use pressure gauges at test ports shown on hydraulic schematic.

title: Genie GS-1930/32 GS-2032/32 GS-2046/46 Service and Repair Manual Part 96316 | Genie Official
content_summary: Official Genie service and repair manual (Part #96316) covering models GS-1930, GS-1932, GS-2032, GS-2632, GS-2046, GS-2646. Published by Genie Industries. Contains: full fault code chart (DTC 01-68 and OIC E1-E13), troubleshooting flowcharts for hydraulic and electrical systems, preventive maintenance schedules, wiring diagrams, hydraulic schematics. GS-1932 specs: 19ft working height, 32in deck width, 500 lb platform capacity, 24V DC electric system, 3.5 mph max drive speed (lowered), 0.5 mph (raised). Hydraulic system: 24V DC motor-driven pump, main relief valve, cylinder lock valve. Battery: four 6V batteries in series for 24V system. Fault code access: platform control box LED display and ground control LED.

title: Common Genie Scissor Lift Error Codes | Aerial Equipment Parts
content_summary: Guide to common Genie scissor lift error codes for GS-1932, GS-3246, and other models. Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System generates Operational Indicator Codes (OIC) and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC). Key OIC codes with solutions: E1 Emergency Stop — disengage E-stop button and retry; E4 Low Battery — charge batteries to at least 80% before operation; E6 Tilt Sensor — move machine to level surface (max slope 1.5°); E12/E13 Control communication fault — inspect control box cable connections. Key DTC codes: 02 platform DIP switch fault, 03 chassis switch stuck at startup, 18 pothole guard failure (check guard deployment mechanism and limit switch), 42-47 solenoid coil faults (forward/reverse/up/down/steer/brake coils — check coil resistance ~12-15Ω), 68 low battery voltage. Full fault code chart in Genie Scissor Service & Repair Manual page 182+. Reset procedure: lower platform fully, power off, use override switch if needed, restart.

title: Genie Scissor Lift Error Codes: Complete Guide 40+ Codes | Flat Earth Equipment
content_summary: Comprehensive Genie scissor lift error code guide with 40+ codes for all GS series models. Genie uses two code types: Operational Indicator Codes (OIC, prefix E) and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC, numeric). Complete OIC list: E1 Emergency Stop active, E2 Recovery Mode active, E4 Low Battery (below operational threshold), E6 Tilt Sensor fault (excessive slope), E7 Platform Overload, E12 Platform-to-Chassis communication loss, E13 Chassis-to-Platform communication loss. Extended DTC codes beyond 68: codes for CAN bus faults, motor controller faults, and sensor calibration faults vary by model generation. Battery voltage requirements by model: GS-1932 requires ≥22V on 24V system; GS-3246 (24V) requires ≥22V; GS-4047 uses 48V system (requires ≥46V). Searchable database covers GS-1532, GS-1932, GS-2032, GS-2632, GS-3246, GS-4047, GS-5390.

title: The Ultimate Guide To The Genie Scissor Lift Fault Codes | Mechnician
content_summary: Comprehensive guide to Genie scissor lift fault codes covering GS-1930, GS-1932, GTH-1056, and GS-3232. Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System (GSDS) with 272 diagnostic trouble codes (DTC) and 6 operation indicator codes (OIC) across five system categories: hydraulic, battery/power, user interface, sensors/switches, and controls. Newer models adding telematics, CAN bus, and Wi-Fi for remote diagnostic access. Most common codes: E1 Emergency Stop, E4 Low Battery, E6 Tilt Sensor, E12/E13 Control Communication. Standard reset procedure: (1) Lower lift completely to ground, (2) Turn off machine, (3) Engage override mode on control panel, (4) Turn machine back on. If code persists after reset, underlying fault must be repaired. Intermittent codes suggest loose connections or developing component failure.

title: JLG Boom Error Codes — FORKLIFT TRUCK Service Manual PDF | ForkliftPDFManuals
content_summary: JLG boom lift error codes reference covering articulating booms (450AJ, 520AJ, 600AJ) and telescopic booms (860SJ, 1200SJ). JLG DTC group structure: Group 1 engine/power, Group 2 drive system, Group 3 platform/boom position, Group 4 sensors, Group 5 CAN communication. Notable codes: 441 platform overload (reduce load), 775 motor controller fault (check motor and controller), 5-437 CAN bus communication fault. Flash code system for pre-CAN models: count LED flashes (first group = main code, second = sub-code). Flash code 3-3 = short circuit in output driver (50+ possible troubleshooting paths). Flash code 4-5 = platform overload. ECT codes: stage 1 warning = reduce engine load; stage 2 = shutdown imminent. Links to PDF service manuals for JLG boom lift models.

title: Manitou Telehandlers: What To Do With Error Codes? | Machineseeker Blog
content_summary: Practical guide to Manitou telehandler error codes and response procedures, relevant to 180ATJ and ATJ series. Manitou error code categories: engine ECU codes (Deutz EURO 3/4/5 codes), hydraulic system codes, transmission/drive codes, safety system codes. Key guidance: when error codes appear constantly or maintenance light flashes — return key to neutral position, wait 10 seconds, restart. For safety system faults, only authorized Manitou Support can resolve — find dealer via manitou.com dealer locator. Common field-resolvable conditions: overload warning (reduce platform load), tilt warning (level machine on flat ground), low fluid level warnings (top up hydraulic fluid or engine oil per specifications). Manitou Maintenance & Diagnostic Software (MDS) required for reading and clearing fault codes on post-2010 machines. Preventive maintenance reduces fault code frequency.

title: DINGLI JCPT2223RTB Operators Manual With Maintenance Information | ManualsLib
content_summary: Dingli JCPT2223RTB operators manual with maintenance information on ManualsLib. JCPT2223RTB: rough terrain self-propelled scissor lift, 22m working height, 2.3m deck width, four-wheel drive. Fault code/error indicator readout section follows same structure as other Dingli JCPT models: codes 10-series (ECU general alarms), 30-series (PCU alarms), 50-series (module-specific alarms: 51 ECU alarm, 52 PCU alarm, 53 TM1 alarm, 54 pressure sensor error, 57 overload, 58 pothole, 59 height limit), 100-series (CAN bus timeouts: 110 ECU watchdog, 111/112 CAN bus timeout). RT model adds codes for 4WD system and rough terrain hydraulics. Maintenance schedule: hydraulic oil check weekly, battery check weekly, pivot pin lubrication monthly, annual hydraulic oil change. Emergency manual descent procedure included.

title: Dingli JCPT0607DCM Operator Manual — Official Dingli PDF
content_summary: Official Dingli operator's manual for JCPT0607DCM (diesel combustion engine variant of JCPT0607 scissor lift). DCM = Diesel Combustion Motor. Platform height 6m, diesel engine powered, designed for outdoor use. Fault code section covers: engine ECU codes (additional to standard Dingli platform codes), platform control codes 10-59 (same as DCS version), hydraulic fault indicators. Additional DCM-specific codes for engine faults: coolant temperature warning, engine oil pressure warning, fuel level warning. Control system fault codes same as JCPT0607DCS: Code 10 ECU alarm, Code 20 TM1 alarm, Code 30-32 PCU alarms, Code 54 pressure sensor error, Code 57 overload, Code 58 pothole protection, Code 59 height limit, Code 110-112 CAN bus timeouts. Contains engine maintenance intervals, hydraulic oil specifications, fuel type requirements.

title: Dingli JCPT1418RT ANSI Operator Manual — Official Dingli PDF
content_summary: Official Dingli JCPT1418RT ANSI operator's manual for North American market. JCPT1418RT: rough terrain scissor lift, 14m working height, 1.8m platform width, all-terrain tires, 4-wheel drive. ANSI A92.6 compliant. Contains: ANSI safety requirements, fault code readout procedure and complete code list, rough terrain operating restrictions, maintenance schedule. Fault codes follow standard Dingli JCPT structure with RT additions: standard platform codes (10-112 series) plus 4WD drive system codes and rough terrain hydraulic codes. Hydraulic system: higher-capacity pump for RT model, outrigger control (if equipped), four-wheel steering option. Battery system: 24V DC or 48V DC depending on variant. Key safety note: rough terrain models must have all stabilizers deployed before platform elevation per ANSI requirements.

title: Diagnosing AWP That Won't Lift: Electrical, Hydraulic, And Lockout Causes | Atomoving
content_summary: Technical guide for diagnosing aerial work platforms that won't lift. Systematic diagnosis approach: Step 1 Lockout causes — check E-stop status, confirm all interlocks satisfied (outriggers deployed if required, tilt sensor not triggered, weight within rated capacity, height limit not reached), verify key switch on. Step 2 Electrical causes — measure battery voltage under load (must meet manufacturer minimum), check control harness continuity, verify pump motor relay operation and motor current draw (stalled motor draws high current). Step 3 Hydraulic causes — check fluid level at reservoir, test pump outlet pressure at test port using pressure gauge (compare to rated pressure in service manual), verify solenoid valve coil resistance (typical 20-40Ω), check solenoid spool movement, perform cylinder internal leak test (raise platform, isolate circuit, watch for drift). Step 4 Read fault codes — use manufacturer analyzer tool or observe LED display. Covers Dingli JCPT series, JLG scissor and boom lifts, Genie GS series.

title: JLG Error Code List: Meaning, Causes & Solutions — BIBERGER
content_summary: Comprehensive JLG error code reference guide covering DTC groups and fault descriptions with meanings, causes, and solutions. JLG fault codes organized by group number: Group 1 engine/power, Group 2 drive system, Group 3 platform/boom positioning, Group 4 sensor faults, Group 5 CAN bus communication. Two-part code identifier: group number + specific fault (e.g., 3-3, 5-437). Flash code reading for older pre-CAN models: count first LED flash group then second group. Key codes covered: engine temperature faults, drive motor controller faults, tilt sensor faults, load cell faults, CAN communication errors. Troubleshooting approach: read DTC, consult service manual table, perform recommended checks, reset after repair. Applies to JLG articulating booms (450AJ, 520AJ, 600AJ), telescopic booms (860SJ, 1200SJ), and scissor lifts (2632ES, 3246ES). German language site with English content sections.

title: Help Descriptions And Fault Flash Codes — JLG E450A Service Manual Page 147 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG E450A (electric 45ft articulating boom) service and maintenance manual page 147 containing help descriptions and fault flash codes. Flash code reading procedure: observe LED indicator on ground control box. Flash codes listed with two-number sequence (e.g., 3-3 = three flashes pause three flashes). Complete fault flash code table: codes organized by system (power, drive, lift, platform, sensors, communications). Each code entry includes: flash sequence, fault description, probable cause, recommended corrective action. The E450A is the electric-powered variant of the JLG 450AJ series. Same flash code structure applies to M450AJ (motor/engine powered). Critical codes: tilt sensor faults, load cell calibration faults, motor controller faults, CAN bus faults. Reset procedure: cycle emergency stop button, cycle key switch.

title: JLG 450AJ Fault Code 3:3 — Platform Troubleshooting | JustAnswer
content_summary: JustAnswer expert discussion about JLG 450AJ fault code 3:3 on the platform control. Fault code 3:3 on JLG 450AJ indicates a short circuit in an output driver — there are at least 50 possible troubleshooting paths for this code. Diagnosis steps: (1) Inspect basket position sensors for dirt, damage, or loose wiring; (2) Check joystick calibration; (3) Ensure all connectors are secure; (4) Reset system by cycling power. Advanced diagnosis: check power and control wiring for shorts in harness and connector pins; if harness tests OK, the controller requires replacement. Note: fault code 3:3 is hardware-related and can originate from any part of the machine. Service manual section for DTC group 3 covers all platform and boom positioning faults. Field tech tip: isolate the issue by disconnecting platform harness and checking if code clears (narrows fault to platform vs. base machine).

title: JLG Fault Codes List, Diagnosis, and Repair | Friday Parts
content_summary: JLG fault codes reference covering DTCs for boom lifts and scissor lifts with diagnosis and repair procedures. JLG DTC structure: first digits indicate system group, remaining digits indicate specific fault within that group. Troubleshooting process: (1) Identify DTC from machine display or analyzer; (2) Refer to service manual DTC table for that model; (3) Follow diagnostic flowchart; (4) Perform corrective action (inspect wiring, replace component, calibrate sensor); (5) Clear code and verify repair. Common repair procedures: wiring harness inspection (check for chafing, corrosion, broken conductors at flex points), connector cleaning and re-pinning, sensor replacement and calibration, controller replacement. Parts compatibility note: JLG controllers and sensors are model-specific — verify part numbers before ordering. Covers JLG 450AJ, 520AJ, 510AJ, 860SJ, and scissor lift models.

title: JLG Fault Codes — Complete Guide & What They Mean | EWP Spares Australia
content_summary: Complete guide to JLG fault codes from EWP Spares Australia, a specialist aerial work platform parts supplier. Covers fault code structure, reading procedures, and common codes with solutions. JLG Analyzer (Part 1001249695) provides full fault description, active and historical faults, and live sensor monitoring. Machine analyzer options: tethered cable, mobile handheld, Bluetooth (select scissor models), remote via ClearSky fleet management. Fault code categories: engine codes (Group 1), drive/travel codes (Group 2), boom/platform codes (Group 3), sensor codes (Group 4), CAN communication codes (Group 5), load management codes (Group 9). Specific codes: 5-437 CAN bus communication fault, 3-3 short circuit in output driver, 4-5 platform overload, 9-2/9-3 load management system faults. Reset: cycle emergency stop or key switch after resolving underlying fault. Applies to JLG 450AJ, 520AJ, 860SJ, and scissor lift models common in Australian market.

title: Detect & Fix Genie Error Codes (Troubleshooting) — BIBERGER
content_summary: Genie scissor lift and boom lift error code troubleshooting guide from Biberger. Covers Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System (GSDS) fault codes for GS series scissor lifts. Operation Indicator Codes (OIC) with E-prefix: E1 Emergency Stop, E2 Recovery Mode, E4 Low Battery, E6 Tilt Sensor, E7 Platform Overload, E12/E13 Communication fault. Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) numeric: 18 Pothole guard, 42-47 solenoid coil faults, 68 Low Voltage. Troubleshooting steps for each code: E4 Low Battery — measure battery voltage (GS-1932: must be ≥22V, GS-3246: ≥22V), charge batteries, check battery connections and individual cell voltages; E6 Tilt — move machine to level surface, check tilt sensor wiring and mounting; Code 18 Pothole guard — inspect guard mechanism deployment and limit switch continuity. Battery voltage requirements by model family. German language site covering European market Genie equipment.

title: Genie GS 3246 Error Code 18 After Limit Switch Install | JustAnswer
content_summary: JustAnswer expert discussion about Genie GS-3246 showing error code 18 after limit switch replacement. Error code 18 = Pothole guard failure — pothole guard not deploying or limit switch not activating correctly. Diagnosis after switch replacement: (1) Verify wiring harness connections to new limit switch; (2) Inspect pothole guard sensor for damage or misalignment; (3) Reset system codes via ground control override; (4) Test limit switch continuity with multimeter (normally open/closed per wiring diagram); (5) Consider controller recalibration if new switch tests OK electrically. Common causes of persistent code 18 after repair: incorrect switch type installed (NO vs NC), harness connector not fully seated, pothole guard arm not returning to fully deployed position. Reference: Genie GS-3246 service manual wiring diagram for pothole guard circuit. Same procedure applies to GS-2046 and GS-2646 models.

title: Manitou Default Fault Codes — Official Manitou Diagnostic Portal
content_summary: Official Manitou Group diagnostic portal listing default fault codes for Manitou equipment including ATJ and aerial work platform series. The portal provides manufacturer-level fault code definitions, causes, and corrective actions for Manitou telehandlers and aerial work platforms. Covers Manitou 180ATJ and other ATJ series machines. Fault code categories: engine ECU codes (Deutz engine variants), hydraulic system codes, safety system codes, transmission codes. This is the official Manitou diagnostic reference — supersedes third-party interpretations. Manitou Maintenance & Diagnostic Software (MDS) required for full code reading and clearing; this portal provides the code reference list for field technicians. Key for 180ATJ: Deutz EURO 3 engine codes overlap with platform control codes — must distinguish between engine ECU faults and Manitou platform controller faults.

title: Genie Scissor Lift GS3246 2003 Model Fault Light Causes and Fixes | JustAnswer
content_summary: JustAnswer expert discussion about a 2003 Genie GS-3246 scissor lift showing fault light. The GS-3246 fault indicator light on the control panel signals an active DTC. Diagnosis steps for 2003 GS-3246 (older model with GEN 5 ECU): (1) Check ground control LED for fault code number; (2) Reference Genie service manual fault code chart for GEN 5; (3) Common faults on older GS-3246: charging system faults (code 52-58), solenoid coil failures (code 42-47 — coils weaken over time), pothole guard wear (code 18). Battery-related issues common on older machines: sulfated batteries causing low voltage codes, charger failure causing persistent low voltage. Maintenance insight: 2003-era Genie GS-3246 uses older hydraulic pump design — pump efficiency decreases with age, may show slow lift even without fault codes. Platform tilt sensor calibration may drift over time.

title: Dingli JCPT1412DC Manuals — Operator and Service Manuals | ManualsLib
content_summary: ManualsLib listing for Dingli JCPT1412DC self-propelled electric scissor lift manuals. The JCPT1412DC is a DC-powered (battery electric) self-propelled scissor lift with 14m working height and 1.2m platform width. Available documentation: Operator's Manual with Maintenance Information (includes fault code list on error indicator readout section). JCPT1412DC fault code system: LED display on ground control box shows two or three digit codes. Code structure same as other Dingli DC models: 10-series ECU alarms, 30-series PCU alarms, 50-series module alarms (overload code 57, pothole code 58, height limit code 59), 100-series CAN bus faults. The DCM (DC Motor controller) variant uses same code set. Hydraulic system: 24V DC motor-driven hydraulic pump. Platform capacity: 230kg (indoor), lower for outdoor/elevated use per rated capacity chart. Dingli service contact for technical support listed in manual.

title: JLG 800S 810SJ 860SJ Parts Manual (3121140) | JLG Official
content_summary: Official JLG parts manual for 800S, 810SJ, and 860SJ telescopic boom lifts (part number 3121140_AL). Serial number range SN 0300000100–0300139999. Published on JLG's official CDN. Contains complete parts breakdown for all major assemblies: boom, turntable, chassis, platform, hydraulic system, electrical system. The 860SJ is an 86ft telescopic boom with dual-fuel engine and 1000 lb platform capacity. Fault codes on the 860SJ follow JLG DTC group structure (see 860SJ Service & Maintenance Manual for full DTC table). Parts reference for 860SJ: boom telescope cylinder seals, boom angle sensor, turntable bearing, outrigger cylinder seals, drive motor, hydraulic pump, ECM/controller modules. Serial number range 0300000100 to 0300139999 covers mid-production 860SJ units. JLG Online Express required for current serial number range documentation.

title: Troubleshooting Tools & Resources | JLG Official
content_summary: Official JLG troubleshooting tools and resources page. JLG offers multiple diagnostic instruments: (1) JLG Analyzer (Part 1001249695) — tethered cable analyzer for reading DTCs, adjusting machine parameters, and live sensor monitoring; (2) Mobile Analyzer — handheld unit for field diagnosis; (3) Bluetooth Analyzer — wireless connection for select scissor lift models; (4) ClearSky Fleet Management — remote telematics for real-time fault monitoring. JLG Knowledge Base (DirectAccess at jlg.com/directaccess) provides searchable database of 1,500+ DTC articles by fault code or symptom — each article includes fault description, possible causes, troubleshooting steps, and special tools required. Online Express portal for service manual downloads by machine serial number. Troubleshooting approach: identify DTC from display or analyzer, look up in service manual DTC table, follow diagnostic flowchart, cycle power after repair to clear code.

title: Genie Lift Connect Inspection Instructions & Troubleshooting Guide | Genie Official
content_summary: Official Genie Industries troubleshooting guide for the Lift Connect telematics system on GS series scissor lifts. Contains: Lift Connect inspection checklist, cellular/GPS connectivity troubleshooting, remote fault code interpretation via Genie cloud platform, alert code list for remotely monitored machines. Genie Lift Connect transmits DTC codes, location, battery status, and usage hours to the Genie platform. Alert codes mirror ground control display DTCs, plus telematics-specific codes for connectivity issues (SIM card, antenna, GPS). Applicable to current production GS-1932, GS-3246, GS-4047, and GS-5390 with factory telematics option. For non-Lift-Connect machines, fault codes are read from machine LED display per service manual fault code chart. Lift Connect enables predictive maintenance by tracking recurring DTC patterns.

title: Genie Z-45 XC Articulating Boom Service Manual (Part 1268197) | Genie Official
content_summary: Official Genie Industries service and repair manual for Z-45 XC articulating boom lift (Part 1268197), serial range from Z4525XCF-101. The Z-45 XC is a diesel/gas articulating boom with 45ft working height and 500 lb capacity. Contains: complete fault code chart for Z-45 XC using Genie SmartLink/GSDS system — DTC codes with descriptions, causes, and corrective actions; hydraulic schematics and troubleshooting flowcharts; electrical wiring diagrams; preventive maintenance schedules; component repair procedures. Fault code structure same as GS scissor series with additional Z-boom codes for: boom angle sensor calibration, jib sensor, platform level sensor, telescope/articulation limit switches. CAN-bus control system with platform and base ECMs. Hydraulic system: engine-driven pump, proportional control valves, load-holding valves on boom cylinders.

title: Genie Z-60 DC Articulating Boom Service Manual (Part 1271125) | Genie Official
content_summary: Official Genie Industries service and repair manual for Z-60 DC articulating boom lift (Part 1271125), serial range from Z6016N-101. The Z-60 DC is a diesel/gas articulating boom with 60ft working height. Contains: fault code chart (DTC and OIC codes for Z-60 DC) with descriptions, causes, and corrective actions; hydraulic troubleshooting for high-reach boom (main relief 3000+ PSI); electrical schematics with CAN-bus control system; telescope and articulation cylinder service procedures; platform rotation system maintenance. Additional fault codes beyond scissor models: telescope extension sensor, secondary boom angle sensor, upper/lower position limit switches. Drive system: four-wheel drive and steer, oscillating axle. Load cell and capacity management system fault codes. Same GSDS fault code framework as Genie GS scissor series with Z-boom extensions.

title: Dingli Machinery Service Manual Download Center | Official Dingli
content_summary: Official Dingli Machinery service manual download hub for all JCPT series scissor lifts and AWP products. Primary source for Dingli technical documentation. Models with documentation available: JCPT0307, JCPT0607 (DCS/DCM variants), JCPT0808, JCPT1008, JCPT1012, JCPT1208, JCPT1412, JCPT1612, JCPT1912, JCPT2223, RT series. Document types: operator's manuals with maintenance info, fault code reference lists, parts catalogs, electrical schematics. Fault code documentation covers DC (battery), DCS (DC Standard), DCM (DC Motor controller), and RT (Rough Terrain) variants — all using the same 10–112 code range with model-specific extensions. Service contact: WeChat official account and email support for models not listed online. This page is the authoritative source for Dingli JCPT1208 and JCPT1412 documentation — key models currently lacking detailed fault code data in the knowledge base.

title: Manitou Fault Codes and Solutions Guide | Scribd PDF
content_summary: Manitou fault codes list and solutions guide PDF on Scribd. Covers Manitou telehandler and aerial work platform fault codes including the 180ATJ series. Contents: engine ECU fault codes (Deutz EURO 3/4 variants, Perkins, Cummins depending on model), hydraulic system fault codes, transmission fault codes, safety system codes, display warning codes. Each entry: code number or symbol, fault description, likely cause, recommended action. Key Manitou AWP codes: overload warning (reduce platform load below rated capacity), tilt/inclination fault (level machine on flat surface), hydraulic pressure fault (check fluid level and pump), jib/boom position sensor faults. For 180ATJ: Deutz ECU codes (engine) are separate from Manitou platform controller codes — must distinguish source. Safety system faults require authorized Manitou technician to clear. Manitou MDS software required to read and clear most DTCs on post-2010 machines.

title: MEC 1330SE Aerial Work Platform Service & Parts Manual (95834) | MEC AWP
content_summary: MEC Aerial Work Platforms service and parts manual for the 1330SE self-propelled electric scissor lift (document 95834, November 2021). MEC is a Canadian manufacturer of AWP equipment common in North American rental fleets. The 1330SE is a 13ft electric indoor scissor lift. Contains: fault code list (LED numeric display codes), hydraulic system diagram, electrical schematics, parts breakdown, maintenance schedule. Fault code structure similar to other scissor brands — ground control box LED shows numeric fault codes. Common MEC 1330SE fault codes cover: battery/charging system faults, drive motor faults, solenoid valve faults, sensor faults (tilt, load). Reset procedure: Emergency Stop button cycle (same as Dingli and Genie scissor lifts). Hydraulic system: 24V DC electric pump, single-acting scissor cylinder with manual lowering valve. ANSI/CSA compliance documentation included.

title: MEC AWP Error Indicator Readout Document (43891-SCH) | MEC AWP
content_summary: MEC Aerial Work Platforms error indicator readout reference document (43891-SCH). Contains the error indicator display reference for MEC AWP scissor lift models. Document covers: how to read the error indicator display, complete list of error codes with descriptions and meanings, troubleshooting steps for each code, reset procedures. Error codes displayed on ground control box LED and/or platform control display. This document was returned alongside Genie and Dingli AWP service documents in fault code searches — MEC error indicator format and reset procedure (Emergency Stop cycle) comparable to other scissor brands. MEC AWP equipment is commonly found in North American fleets alongside Genie and JLG equipment. This document is useful for technicians transitioning between brands as comparative reference.

title: JLG Control System Section 6 — CAN Bus Troubleshooting Manual (3121171)
content_summary: JLG control system troubleshooting manual section 6 (reference 3121171) covering the JLG CAN-bus control system used in boom lift models. This document provides in-depth troubleshooting for JLG electrical/control system architecture applicable to articulating booms (450AJ, 520AJ, 600AJ) and telescopic booms (800S, 860SJ) using the same CAN-bus platform. Section 6 covers: CAN bus wiring topology and node identification, controller module identification (Ground ECM, Platform ECM, Engine Control Module), diagnostic analyzer connection points, DTC reading and clearing via JLG Analyzer, controller replacement and programming procedures, CAN bus fault isolation methodology. Wiring fault diagnosis: step-by-step process for identifying open circuits, shorts, and intermittent connections in CAN bus harness. ECM replacement guide: programming new controllers, CAN network address assignment. Critical reference for diagnosing JLG 860SJ communication fault codes (Group 5: e.g., 5-437 CAN bus fault).
